steps.title: Locate the stated thesis
steps.content: The passage explicitly identifies the cause of Izquierdo's obscurity: "One reason for Izquierdo's relative obscurity is the enormous popularity of some of her peers"—namely Kahlo and Rivera, who "captivated the interest of US audiences" so much that Izquierdo gets "overlooked."

steps.title: Define what the main idea must contain
steps.content: Any correct main-idea answer must capture two elements: (1) Izquierdo is not well-known in the US, and (2) the cause is Kahlo and Rivera drawing public attention away from her. Anything that adds new facts, reverses the relationship, or substitutes a different cause is wrong.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — Izquierdo's work is not as well-known in the United States as it should be because Kahlo and Rivera draw so much of the public's attention.: directly restates both required elements. ✓
  • Choice B — During Izquierdo's lifetime, her paintings were displayed in galleries in the United States much more frequently than paintings by Kahlo and Rivera were.: reverses the relationship and invents gallery-display data the passage never mentions. ✗
  • Choice C — Izquierdo painted some of the same subjects that Kahlo and Rivera painted but used different techniques than they used.: overreach—the passage says nothing about shared subjects or technique differences. ✗
  • Choice D — Few of Izquierdo's works are in galleries today because she produced only a small number of paintings.: substitutes a different cause (low output) that the passage never claims; the passage attributes obscurity to peers' popularity, not scarcity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
